sentences.en: Artificial intelligence (AI) can already predict the future.
sentences.zh: 人工智能（AI）已经能够预测未来。

grammar.type: 简单句
grammar.note: Artificial intelligence (AI) 为主语；can already predict the future 为谓语；already 意为 “已经”；predict 意为 “预测”。

sentences.en: Police forces are using it to map when and where crime is likely to occur. Doctors can use it to predict when a patient is most likely to have a heart attack or stroke.
sentences.zh: 警方正利用它来标定犯罪最可能发生的时间和地点。医生可以用它来预测病人最有可能在何时突发心脏病或中风。

grammar.type: 不定式目的 + when/where 宾语从句
grammar.note: are using it to map when and where crime is likely to occur 中 to map 表目的，when and where... 为宾语从句；can use it to predict when... 同理；be likely to 意为 “有可能”；heart attack 意为 “心脏病发作”，stroke 意为 “中风”。

sentences.en: Researchers are even trying to give AI imagination so it can plan for unexpected consequences.
sentences.zh: 研究人员甚至试图赋予人工智能想象力，以便它能为意料之外的后果做出规划。

grammar.type: so (that) 目的从句
grammar.note: are even trying to give AI imagination 为主句；so it can plan for unexpected consequences 为 so (that) 引导的目的状语从句；plan for 意为 “为……做规划”；unexpected 意为 “意料之外的”。

sentences.en: Many decisions in our lives require a good forecast, and AI is almost always better at forecasting than we are.
sentences.zh: 我们生活中的许多决定都需要一个好的预测，而人工智能在预测方面几乎总是比我们做得更好。

grammar.type: and 并列 + 比较
grammar.note: 两个分句由 and 并列；AI is almost always better at forecasting than we are 为比较结构，be better at 意为 “更擅长”；forecast 作名词意为 “预测”；require 意为 “需要”。

sentences.en: Yet for all these technological advances, we still seem to deeply lack confidence in AI predictions.
sentences.zh: 然而，尽管有这些技术进步，我们似乎仍然对人工智能的预测严重缺乏信心。

grammar.type: for all 让步
grammar.note: Yet 表转折；for all these technological advances 为介词短语表让步，意为 “尽管有……”；we still seem to deeply lack confidence 为主句；lack confidence in 意为 “对……缺乏信心”。

sentences.en: Recent cases show that people don’t like relying on AI and prefer to trust human experts, even if these experts are wrong.
sentences.zh: 近期的一些案例表明，人们不喜欢依赖人工智能，而更愿意信任人类专家，即使这些专家是错的。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句 + even if 让步
grammar.note: show that people don't like relying on AI and prefer to trust... 为宾语从句，含并列谓语；even if these experts are wrong 为 even if 让步从句；rely on 意为 “依赖”，prefer to 意为 “更愿意”。

sentences.en: If we want AI to really benefit people, we need to find a way to get people to trust it.
sentences.zh: 如果我们想让人工智能真正造福于人，就需要找到一种让人们信任它的办法。

grammar.type: if 条件从句 + get sb to do
grammar.note: If we want AI to really benefit people 为条件从句，want sb to do 结构；主句 we need to find a way to get people to trust it；get sb to do 意为 “让某人做”；benefit 意为 “造福、有益于”。

sentences.en: To do that, we need to understand why people are so reluctant to trust AI in the first place.
sentences.zh: 要做到这一点，我们首先需要弄明白人们为何如此不愿信任人工智能。

grammar.type: 不定式状语 + why 宾语从句
grammar.note: To do that 为不定式表目的；understand why people are so reluctant to trust AI 为 why 宾语从句；in the first place 意为 “首先、一开始”；be reluctant to 意为 “不愿”。

sentences.en: Take the case of Watson for Oncology, one of technology giant IBM’s supercomputer programs.
sentences.zh: 以“沃森肿瘤学系统”为例，它是科技巨头IBM的超级计算机程序之一。

grammar.type: 祈使句 + 同位语
grammar.note: Take the case of... 为祈使句，意为 “以……为例”；one of technology giant IBM's supercomputer programs 为 Watson for Oncology 的同位语；oncology 意为 “肿瘤学”，giant 意为 “巨头”。

sentences.en: Their attempt to promote this program to cancer doctors was a PR disaster.
sentences.zh: 他们试图向癌症医生推广这一程序的尝试，却成了一场公关灾难。

grammar.type: 不定式定语
grammar.note: Their attempt to promote this program to cancer doctors 为主语，to promote... 为不定式作定语修饰 attempt；was a PR disaster 为系表结构；PR 为 public relations（公关）的缩写；disaster 意为 “灾难”。

sentences.en: The AI promised to deliver top-quality recommendations on the treatment of 12 cancers that accounted for 80% of the world’s cases.
sentences.zh: 这套人工智能承诺就12种癌症的治疗提供顶级建议，而这12种癌症占了全球病例的80%。

grammar.type: that 定语从句
grammar.note: promised to deliver top-quality recommendations on... 为主句，promise to do 意为 “承诺做”；that accounted for 80% of the world's cases 为 that 定语从句修饰 cancers；account for 意为 “占（比例）”；top-quality 意为 “顶级的”。

sentences.en: But when doctors first interacted with Watson, they found themselves in a rather difficult situation.
sentences.zh: 但当医生们最初与沃森互动时，他们发现自己陷入了相当为难的境地。

grammar.type: when 时间从句
grammar.note: when doctors first interacted with Watson 为时间状语从句；主句 they found themselves in a rather difficult situation；find oneself in 意为 “发觉自己处于”；interact with 意为 “与……互动”，rather 意为 “相当”。

sentences.en: On the one hand, if Watson provided guidance about a treatment that coincided with their own opinions, physicians did not see much point in Watson’s recommendations.
sentences.zh: 一方面，如果沃森提供的治疗指导与医生自己的看法一致，医生就觉得沃森的建议没有太大意义。

grammar.type: On the one hand + if 条件从句 + that 定语从句
grammar.note: On the one hand 意为 “一方面”；if Watson provided guidance about a treatment that coincided with... 为条件从句，含 that 定语从句；主句 physicians did not see much point in...；see point in 意为 “看出……的意义”，coincide with 意为 “与……一致”。

sentences.en: The supercomputer was simply telling them what they already knew, and these recommendations did not change the actual treatment.
sentences.zh: 这台超级计算机不过是在告诉他们已经知道的东西，而这些建议并没有改变实际的治疗方案。

grammar.type: what 宾语从句 + and 并列
grammar.note: was simply telling them what they already knew 中 what they already knew 为宾语从句；and these recommendations did not change the actual treatment 为并列分句；simply 意为 “只不过”，actual 意为 “实际的”。

sentences.en: On the other hand, if Watson generated a recommendation that contradicted the experts’ opinion, doctors would typically conclude that Watson wasn’t competent.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，如果沃森给出的建议与专家的意见相矛盾，医生通常就会断定沃森不够称职。

grammar.type: On the other hand + if 条件从句 + that 宾语从句
grammar.note: On the other hand 意为 “另一方面”；if Watson generated a recommendation that contradicted... 为条件从句，含 that 定语从句；主句 doctors would typically conclude that Watson wasn't competent，含 that 宾语从句；contradict 意为 “与……矛盾”，competent 意为 “称职的”。

sentences.en: And the machine wouldn’t be able to explain why its treatment was plausible because its machine-learning algorithms were simply too complex to be fully understood by humans.
sentences.zh: 而且这台机器无法解释它给出的治疗方案为何合理，因为它的机器学习算法实在太复杂，人类无法完全理解。

grammar.type: why 宾语从句 + because 原因 + too...to...
grammar.note: wouldn't be able to explain why its treatment was plausible 含 why 宾语从句；because its machine-learning algorithms were simply too complex to be fully understood 为原因从句，含 too...to... 结构（被动）；plausible 意为 “貌似合理的”，algorithm 意为 “算法”。

sentences.en: Consequently, this has caused even more suspicion and disbelief, leading many doctors to ignore the seemingly outlandish AI recommendations and stick to their own expertise.
sentences.zh: 因此，这引发了更多的怀疑和不信任，导致许多医生无视看似离谱的人工智能建议，转而坚持自己的专业判断。

grammar.type: 现在分词状语 + lead sb to do
grammar.note: Consequently 意为 “因此”；this has caused even more suspicion and disbelief 为主句；leading many doctors to ignore... and stick to... 为现在分词作结果状语，lead sb to do 含两个并列不定式；outlandish 意为 “离奇的”，stick to 意为 “坚持”。

sentences.en: This is just one example of people’s lack of confidence in AI and their reluctance to accept what AI has to offer.
sentences.zh: 这只是人们对人工智能缺乏信心、不愿接受人工智能所能提供之物的一个例子。

grammar.type: what 宾语从句
grammar.note: This is just one example of... 为主系表；people's lack of confidence in AI and their reluctance to accept... 为并列的介词宾语；what AI has to offer 为 accept 的宾语从句；reluctance 意为 “不情愿”，have to offer 意为 “能提供”。

sentences.en: Trust in other people is often based on our understanding of how others think and having experience of their reliability.
sentences.zh: 对他人的信任往往建立在我们对他人思维方式的理解、以及对其可靠性的亲身体验之上。

grammar.type: be based on + 并列动名词
grammar.note: be based on 意为 “建立在……之上”；our understanding of how others think 与 having experience of their reliability 为并列的介词宾语（一名词短语、一动名词短语），how others think 为宾语从句；reliability 意为 “可靠性”。

sentences.en: This helps create a psychological feeling of safety. AI, on the other hand, is still fairly new and unfamiliar to most people.
sentences.zh: 这有助于营造一种心理上的安全感。而人工智能对大多数人来说，仍然相当新鲜和陌生。

grammar.type: help do + on the other hand 对比
grammar.note: This helps create a psychological feeling of safety 中 help (to) create 为 help do 结构；AI, on the other hand, is still fairly new and unfamiliar 为对比分句，on the other hand 作插入语；psychological 意为 “心理的”，unfamiliar 意为 “陌生的”。

sentences.en: Even if it can be technically explained (and that’s not always the case), AI’s decision-making process is usually too difficult for most people to comprehend.
sentences.zh: 即使它能从技术上得到解释（而情况也并非总是如此），人工智能的决策过程对大多数人来说通常仍太难以理解。

grammar.type: Even if 让步 + 插入 + too...for...to...
grammar.note: Even if it can be technically explained 为让步从句；(and that's not always the case) 为插入补充；主句 AI's decision-making process is usually too difficult for most people to comprehend 为 too...for sb to... 结构；comprehend 意为 “理解”，technically 意为 “技术上”。

sentences.en: And interacting with something we don’t understand can cause anxiety and give us a sense that we’re losing control.
sentences.zh: 而与我们不理解的东西打交道，会引发焦虑，并让我们产生一种正在失去控制的感觉。

grammar.type: 动名词主语 + 省略关系词定语从句 + 同位语从句
grammar.note: interacting with something we don't understand 为动名词短语作主语，(that) we don't understand 为省略关系词的定语从句；can cause anxiety and give us a sense that... 为并列谓语；that we're losing control 为 sense 的同位语从句；anxiety 意为 “焦虑”。

sentences.en: Many people are also simply not familiar with many instances of AI actually working, because it often happens in the background.
sentences.zh: 许多人也根本不熟悉人工智能实际发挥作用的诸多实例，因为它往往是在后台运行的。

grammar.type: because 原因从句 + 现在分词定语
grammar.note: be not familiar with 意为 “不熟悉”；many instances of AI actually working 中 actually working 为现在分词作定语修饰 AI（动名词复合结构）；because it often happens in the background 为原因从句；in the background 意为 “在后台”。

sentences.en: Instead, they are acutely aware of instances where AI goes wrong.
sentences.zh: 相反，他们对人工智能出错的实例却格外敏感。

grammar.type: where 定语从句
grammar.note: Instead 意为 “相反”；be acutely aware of 意为 “敏锐/格外意识到”；instances where AI goes wrong 中 where 引导定语从句修饰 instances；go wrong 意为 “出错”，acutely 意为 “敏锐地”。

sentences.en: Embarrassing AI failures receive a disproportionate amount of media attention, emphasising the message that we cannot rely on technology.
sentences.zh: 令人尴尬的人工智能失误会得到过多的媒体关注，从而强化了“我们不能依赖技术”这一信息。

grammar.type: 现在分词状语 + that 同位语从句
grammar.note: Embarrassing AI failures receive a disproportionate amount of media attention 为主句；emphasising the message that... 为现在分词作结果状语，that we cannot rely on technology 为 message 的同位语从句；disproportionate 意为 “不成比例的、过多的”。

sentences.en: Machine learning is not foolproof, in part because the humans who design it aren’t.
sentences.zh: 机器学习并非万无一失，部分原因在于设计它的人类本身也并非如此。

grammar.type: in part because + who 定语从句 + 省略
grammar.note: Machine learning is not foolproof 为主句；in part because the humans who design it aren't 为原因从句，who design it 为定语从句，aren't 后省略 foolproof；foolproof 意为 “万无一失的”，in part 意为 “部分地”。

sentences.en: Feelings about AI run deep.
sentences.zh: 人们对人工智能的感受十分深刻。

grammar.type: 简单句
grammar.note: Feelings about AI 为主语；run deep 为谓语，意为 “根深蒂固、十分深刻”；run 此处为系动词性用法，接形容词 deep。

sentences.en: In a recent experiment, people from a range of backgrounds were given various sci-fi films about AI to watch and then asked questions about automation in everyday life.
sentences.zh: 在最近的一项实验中，来自不同背景的人被给予各种关于人工智能的科幻电影观看，然后被问及有关日常生活中自动化的问题。

grammar.type: 被动 + 不定式 + 并列被动
grammar.note: were given various sci-fi films... to watch 为被动，to watch 为不定式作定语/目的；and then (were) asked questions about... 为并列被动谓语；a range of 意为 “一系列、各种”，sci-fi 意为 “科幻的”，automation 意为 “自动化”。

sentences.en: It was found that, regardless of whether the film they watched depicted AI in a positive or negative light, simply watching a cinematic vision of our technological future polarised the participants’ attitudes.
sentences.zh: 研究发现，无论他们所看的电影是把人工智能描绘得正面还是负面，仅仅观看一幅关于我们技术未来的电影化图景，就会使参与者的态度两极分化。

grammar.type: it 形式主语 + regardless of whether 让步 + 动名词主语
grammar.note: It was found that... 为形式主语句；regardless of whether the film... depicted AI in a positive or negative light 为让步状语，含 whether 从句；simply watching a cinematic vision... 为动名词主语；polarise 意为 “使两极分化”，cinematic 意为 “电影的”。

sentences.en: Optimists became more extreme in their enthusiasm for AI and sceptics became even more guarded.
sentences.zh: 乐观者对人工智能的热情变得更加极端，而怀疑者则变得更加谨慎戒备。

grammar.type: and 并列 + 比较
grammar.note: Optimists became more extreme... and sceptics became even more guarded 为 and 连接的并列句，两处均含比较级；optimist 意为 “乐观者”，sceptic 意为 “怀疑者”，guarded 意为 “谨慎戒备的”。

sentences.en: This suggests people use relevant evidence about AI in a biased manner to support their existing attitudes, a deep-rooted human tendency known as “confirmation bias”.
sentences.zh: 这表明人们会以带有偏见的方式利用有关人工智能的相关证据，来支持自己已有的态度——这是一种被称为“确认偏误”的根深蒂固的人类倾向。

grammar.type: 省略that宾语从句 + 同位语 + 过去分词定语
grammar.note: suggests (that) people use relevant evidence... in a biased manner to support... 为省略 that 的宾语从句；a deep-rooted human tendency 为前面整句的同位语；known as 'confirmation bias' 为过去分词定语；biased 意为 “有偏见的”，confirmation bias 意为 “确认偏误”。

sentences.en: As AI is represented more and more in media and entertainment, it could lead to a society split between those who benefit from AI and those who reject it.
sentences.zh: 随着人工智能在媒体和娱乐中越来越多地被呈现，它可能导致社会分裂为从人工智能中获益的人和排斥它的人。

grammar.type: As 时间从句 + 过去分词定语 + who 定语从句
grammar.note: As AI is represented more and more... 为 as 时间状语从句；主句 it could lead to a society split between... and...；split between... 为过去分词定语修饰 society；两个 those who... 为定语从句；represent 意为 “呈现、代表”。

sentences.en: More pertinently, refusing to accept the advantages offered by AI could place a large group of people at a serious disadvantage.
sentences.zh: 更为切题的是，拒绝接受人工智能所带来的种种好处，可能会使一大群人处于严重的不利地位。

grammar.type: 动名词主语 + 过去分词定语
grammar.note: More pertinently 为评注状语；refusing to accept the advantages offered by AI 为动名词短语作主语，offered by AI 为过去分词定语；place sb at a disadvantage 意为 “使某人处于不利地位”；pertinently 意为 “切题地”。

sentences.en: Fortunately, we already have some ideas about how to improve trust in AI.
sentences.zh: 幸运的是，我们对于如何增进对人工智能的信任已经有了一些想法。

grammar.type: how to do 宾语
grammar.note: Fortunately 为评注状语；have some ideas about how to improve trust in AI 中 how to improve... 为 “疑问词+不定式” 作 about 的宾语；improve trust in 意为 “增进对……的信任”。

sentences.en: Simply having previous experience with AI can significantly improve people’s opinions about the technology, as was found in the study mentioned above.
sentences.zh: 正如上文提到的研究所发现的那样，仅仅拥有以往与人工智能打交道的经验，就能显著改善人们对这项技术的看法。

grammar.type: 动名词主语 + as 非限制性从句
grammar.note: Simply having previous experience with AI 为动名词短语作主语；can significantly improve people's opinions 为谓语；as was found in the study mentioned above 为 as 引导的非限制性定语从句，mentioned above 为过去分词定语；significantly 意为 “显著地”。

sentences.en: Evidence also suggests the more you use other technologies such as the internet, the more you trust them.
sentences.zh: 有证据还表明，你越是使用互联网等其他技术，就越是信任它们。

grammar.type: 省略that宾语从句 + the+比较级...the+比较级...
grammar.note: suggests (that)... 为省略 that 的宾语从句；the more you use..., the more you trust them 为 “the+比较级..., the+比较级...” 结构，表 “越……越……”；such as the internet 为举例。

sentences.en: Another solution may be to reveal more about the algorithms which AI uses and the purposes they serve.
sentences.zh: 另一个解决办法或许是更多地公开人工智能所使用的算法以及它们所服务的目的。

grammar.type: 不定式表语 + which/省略关系词定语从句
grammar.note: Another solution may be to reveal more about... 中 to reveal 为不定式作表语；the algorithms which AI uses 含 which 定语从句；the purposes (which) they serve 含省略关系词的定语从句；reveal 意为 “揭示、公开”，serve 意为 “服务于”。

sentences.en: Several high-profile social media companies and online marketplaces already release transparency reports about government requests and surveillance disclosures.
sentences.zh: 若干知名的社交媒体公司和在线交易平台已经发布关于政府数据索取和监控披露的透明度报告。

grammar.type: 简单句并列主语
grammar.note: Several high-profile social media companies and online marketplaces 为并列主语；already release transparency reports about... 为谓语；high-profile 意为 “引人注目的、知名的”，transparency 意为 “透明度”，surveillance 意为 “监控”。

sentences.en: A similar practice for AI could help people have a better understanding of the way algorithmic decisions are made.
sentences.zh: 针对人工智能采取类似的做法，可以帮助人们更好地理解算法决策是如何做出的。

grammar.type: help sb do + the way 定语从句
grammar.note: A similar practice for AI 为主语；could help people have a better understanding of... 为 help sb do 结构；the way algorithmic decisions are made 中含省略 that/in which 的定语从句；algorithmic 意为 “算法的”。

sentences.en: Research suggests that allowing people some control over AI decision-making could also improve trust and enable AI to learn from human experience.
sentences.zh: 研究表明，让人们对人工智能的决策拥有一定的控制权，也能够增进信任，并使人工智能能够从人类经验中学习。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句 + 动名词主语
grammar.note: suggests that... 为宾语从句；allowing people some control over AI decision-making 为动名词短语作从句主语；could also improve trust and enable AI to learn... 为并列谓语，enable sb to do 结构；control over 意为 “对……的控制”。

sentences.en: For example, one study showed that when people were allowed the freedom to slightly modify an algorithm, they felt more satisfied with its decisions, more likely to believe it was superior and more likely to use it in the future.
sentences.zh: 例如，一项研究表明，当人们被允许自由地对算法稍作修改时，他们会对它的决策更满意，更倾向于相信它更优越，也更有可能在未来使用它。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句 + when 时间从句 + 三项并列
grammar.note: showed that... 为宾语从句；when people were allowed the freedom to slightly modify an algorithm 为时间从句；主句 they felt more satisfied..., more likely to believe..., and more likely to use... 含三项并列表语；modify 意为 “修改”，be satisfied with 意为 “对……满意”。

sentences.en: We don’t need to understand the intricate inner workings of AI systems, but if people are given a degree of responsibility for how they are implemented, they will be more willing to accept AI into their lives.
sentences.zh: 我们并不需要理解人工智能系统错综复杂的内部运作，但如果人们对这些系统如何被应用被赋予一定程度的责任，他们就会更愿意把人工智能接纳进自己的生活。

grammar.type: but 转折 + if 条件从句 + how 宾语从句
grammar.note: We don't need to understand... 为主句；but if people are given a degree of responsibility for... 为条件从句，含被动；how they are implemented 为 for 的宾语从句；主句 they will be more willing to accept AI；intricate 意为 “错综复杂的”，implement 意为 “实施、应用”。

questions.title: Questions 27–32
instructions: Reading Passage 3 has six sections, A–F.
Choose the correct heading for each section from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–viii, in boxes 27–32 on your answer sheet.
i An increasing divergence of attitudes towards AI
ii Reasons why we have more faith in human judgement than in AI
iii The superiority of AI projections over those made by humans
iv The process by which AI can help us make good decisions
v The advantages of involving users in AI processes
vi Widespread distrust of an AI innovation
vii Encouraging openness about how AI functions
viii A surprisingly successful AI application

questions.title: Questions 33–35
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 33–35 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt:
What is the writer doing in Section A?
A providing a solution to a concern
B justifying an opinion about an issue
C highlighting the existence of a problem
D explaining the reasons for a phenomenon

According to Section C, why might some people be reluctant to accept AI?
A They are afraid it will replace humans in decision-making jobs.
B Its complexity makes them feel that they are at a disadvantage.
C They would rather wait for the technology to be tested over a period of time.
D Misunderstandings about how it works make it seem more challenging than it is.

What does the writer say about the media in Section C of the text?
A It leads the public to be mistrustful of AI.
B It devotes an excessive amount of attention to AI.
C Its reports of incidents involving AI are often inaccurate.
D It gives the impression that AI failures are due to designer error.

questions.title: Questions 36–40
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 36–40 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

items.prompt: Subjective depictions of AI in sci-fi films make people change their opinions about automation.
Portrayals of AI in media and entertainment are likely to become more positive.
Rejection of the possibilities of AI may have a negative effect on many people’s lives.
Familiarity with AI has very little impact on people’s attitudes to the technology.
AI applications which users are able to modify are more likely to gain consumer approval.
